And I'm a wrestler guy who's trying to learn jujitsu. And he came over to beat me up for a while. And he came in. He walked in. He's like, whoa, this is like funnel catcher. And I was laughing so hard. I'm like, that's the funniest thing in the world. And so then later on, after he choked me out at least five or six times, it was really embarrassing. And I kept on admitting this on stage. Yeah, he choked me out a whole bunch of times. Then we were just kind of talking. And I was thinking about that, like, about this concept. And we were talking about jujitsu and wrestling. And it's probably the same in most sports. But in wrestling, there's only a couple core ways that I take somebody down. When I'm out there wrestling somebody, I'm either going to do a single leg, a double leg, or a high C. That's kind of it. And there's other things. There's throws. There's other things you can do. But if you look at wrestling, especially the higher level, you get to college, you get to the Olympic level, the world level. Almost every match, it's one of these three takedowns. And I was thinking, when I was wrestling, we were doing three, four hour practices a day for 12 or 13 years to learn three moves. But it's just three moves. It doesn't really make much sense. But then, and Nick was talking about that with jujitsu, he's like, there's only like, there's the core moves you do. But he said, but the cool thing is that to get in those moves, there's a whole bunch of setups. Like in wrestling for a single leg, there's probably 20 or 30 different ways that I have to get somebody to move to be able to get a single leg. There's 20 or 30 different ways that I move someone and get them set up and I'm trying to do things to get in there. And so wrestling practice is all about learning out different ways to open somebody up so you can get the different takedowns. And I was thinking about that, and I was like, this is so similar to what we all do in our businesses. The hot topic for the last two years, I think in the internet marketing has been funnels. And there's everyone talking about all sorts of stuff. But if you look at it, if you boil down funnel psychology or whatever, there's only a couple real core funnels. The core funnels that we use probably 90 ups in the time are either a tripwire funnel, a webinar funnel, or a high ticket funnel. Like that's it. There's other things you can do, just like in wrestling. There's throws I can do and there's a bunch of other things. But the core breaks down to that. Those are the core things. That's the foundation. That's the basics of internet marketing. But to get people in to each of these funnels, there's probably 20 or 30 different ways to get people into each of these different funnels. And it's been interesting watching, especially over the last probably 18 months or so. And I saw it initially when I first got started. When I first got started, I got started 12 years ago. And we've been doing this for at least 12 years, a couple people. So when I got started 12 years ago, everyone got traffic from one spot. Anyone have an idea what that is? It was Google. Like that was it. You get online, you create a product, and then you go to Google and you buy ads. Like that was it. That was internet marketing as a whole. And people were making crazy amounts of money. I was like, this is so cool. This guy named Chris Carpenter came out of a book called Google Cash. And it was just like, everyone read that book. And I'm like, I can sell anything. I just go to Google, buy some ads, and you're rich. Like that was how hard internet marketing was 12 years ago. I'm like, sweet. So I was in there trying to figure it out. And probably a year into my career, whatever you want to call it, the big Google slap came about. And they didn't hear about that. People were making $1 million a month. They got slapped. They got kicked off Google and just gone. Business was gone overnight. It was just gone. And so then everyone's freaking out. And we're all stressing out. Trying to figure out, what do we do? Like Google's gone. They screwed us all over. Like what do we do now? And so we're trying all sorts of things. And there are a lot of things people were trying back then. But all of them seemed like they were based around some type of free search, organic search, SEO. And so we figured out something. OK, this is working. You start working, you start making a bunch of money, and then all of a sudden, boom, the next Google slap came about. They got Panda and Penguin and Hummingbird. All these different slaps. And every slap is like, we're making a bunch of money. And it's like, oh, we just took it all away. We figure out, make a bunch of money, boom, take it all away. And so I've been watching this for a long, long time. It keeps happening. It keeps happening. And so in my business, I've had a couple of times where we've built our business really, really big. And then we've been slapped either by a traffic slap, or we had a merchant account slap a few years ago that was really bad. And I've always been very paranoid. One thing in a business stresses me out. Like, one traffic source, one merchant account, one of anything freaks me out. I was in an event last week, and James Sramko was talking. And he said, I see something about, he called it the, in business, you have the NOAA, he's called the NOAA principle, I think he called it or something. We're basically like, when NOAA's on the arc, right, you brought in two animals. Like, everything is like, in your business, you've got to have redundancy everywhere. Otherwise, you're kind of in trouble. And so we've been focusing for the last few years on all these different ways to get traffic. That's always been our focus. And then something positive and negative happened. This thing called Facebook came out, right? And Facebook was easy. It was like Google when it first came out. It was easy. It was like awesome. And everyone started jumping and starting making a bunch of money, right? And everyone was like, sweet. And they just did only Facebook. How many guys, 100% of your traffic right now comes from Facebook? How many want to admit that? How many, I'm saying 90% of your traffic comes from Facebook? The majority comes from Facebook. Who's got no traffic coming to the site? OK? It's because it was easy, because working, everyone was there, right? I'm watching it as a group as a whole, right? Everyone, like, they get their thing up. And I'm talking about all these things that we do, Dream 100, and all these things. And everyone's like, OK, cool. I'm just going to do Facebook ads, right? Oh my god. And then the problem is, because Facebook was so awesome, everyone's been rewarded by that behavior. So they're like, oh, we should do Facebook ads. And so it's been awesome, right? And so it's been good. A lot of people made a lot of money on just primarily Facebook, right? But the biggest problem that I'm kind of seeing, and I'm not sure about all your guys' businesses, but in January this year, Facebook started doing what Google started doing, right? The Facebook slap is happening. I think since January this year, we probably lost, man, I would say at least 20 different accounts that Facebook has shut down. We've had accounts where we set it up, we get the Facebook rep to go and write the ads for us, set up the ads for us. They designed everything. They did everything, and we thought, run. And it runs for like a week, and then they shut it down. And we're like, dude, you're the one that did this. He's like, I don't know. I'm like, how does the dude inside of Facebook do our whole thing and still get shut down? Like, how is that even possible, right? And I think we're going through that again right now. Like, the Facebook slap's happening, and stuff is changing, OK? And it hasn't forever when you have. Some people are still running, some people make a bunch of money. But the future, I've been down this path so many times over the next 12 years, I know what the future is, right? The way networks work is they want you to come in, they make it really easy, so everybody comes, dumps their money in, and then they kick out all those direct response marketers. Like, they don't like us. We're not the people they want. So they kick us out, and so it's happening. It's happening at Facebook level, and it's going to continue to happen. And so I think now is a very, very appropriate time to start talking about this. OK, now that this thing was super easy, it's kind of like, not coming to an end, but it's changing, now it's time to start shifting our focus back again to, what are all the things we can do to get people in our funnel? Because I don't want you guys relying on Facebook. How many of you guys have got an account deleted by Facebook in the last eight or nine months? Look around, you guys. That's almost, that's a lot. If it hasn't happened yet, it probably will. And so if all your eggs are in the Facebook basket, it's kind of scary. Now there's strategies within Facebook we're going to talk about as well that'll help protect you. But I want you guys to be aware of that. Just like in wrestling, I don't want to have one move. There's a guy that wrestles me at BYU. The beginning of the season, he was ranked number one in the nation, and he was awesome. He had one of the best high seas I'd ever seen. His name was Rangi, and he was just awesome. Like, he got everybody on it. And I was a freshman there, branding out of high school, and the first two or three months, he just beat me bad every single day. But I started watching him at all his matches. And I started noticing that this is what he'd do. He'd come right here, and he'd keep his hand behind his back, and he'd just wait for you to reach. And as soon as you reach, he'd hit right here, and he'd shoot. And that was his only setup. He had one setup to the thing. But he was amazing at that one thing, right? And so I started watching him. I'm like, that's really, really weird. This guy's ranked number one in the country. I'm a brand new freshman coming in. He's beating me. I'm like, I wonder what would happen if I didn't reach. I wonder what would happen if I let it with my other leg, how that would change things. So I changed my leg, and I let it with the other hand. He couldn't take me down anymore. The best kid in the country could not be a kid right at high school. He just changed the whole dynamics. That took away his one take down. He only had one. And I watched him in the year. At first year, he was beating everyone, beating everyone, beating everyone. And then in the middle of the year, guest service started noticing, say the thing I did. Rengi only has one take down. That's all he knows how to do. By then the year, he didn't even place it at the national tournament. And so that's where I think a lot of our businesses are right now, where we're really good at one thing. It's working, it's working, it's working. And that's good. But now is the time to start developing more talents, more skills, more setups to get people into your funnels. And so that's kind of what today's about. Is that how you guys excited? Cool. Awesome. I'm excited too. So that's what Funnel Catchers all about. All right. OK, now the question was, why does Google and Facebook, why do they do that? And the real reason is the people they want to give them money are brands like Ford and Toyota and McDonald's, people who ride them a check for $1 billion and not care what happens. People like us are so annoying to them because what's our goal? We want to take the person off their platform, take them over here, and then we're doing all these things to them, which are good marketing tech. It's all good stuff. But it doesn't serve them. And so they usually, today's the day they open the doors because direct response market is the one that initially bring people in and bring the eyeballs and get stuff. You know, everyone's creating courses on how to do it, and you get this big huge influx. And eyeballs start coming, people start coming, and they leverage that initially to launch networks. Like I've seen it over and over and over again. And after they've got that, then the big brands want to come because they're saying, wow, these people are making money, or big brands start coming in. And then they're like, sweet, now we can get rid of all these annoying direct response guys who just take our customers and sell them stuff. And that's kind of the thing. And that's why Google, a lot of my friends used to do ad agencies for guys like us. And now they're like, always do direct response guys. And I think it's nuts, or branding guys. I think it's nuts about that. So one of my friends, he got a big huge account. And for most of us, if we're hiring someone to drive traffic for us, we're saying, OK, my cost per acquisition is this. You got to keep it below this. And we have stats and numbers we want. So the stats and numbers for a McDonald's, or a Ford, or Chevrolet, or a big company like that, this is what they come to and said, the only metric they care about is impressions. At the end of the month, I needed you to show me how many times our ads were displayed. That's it. That's the only metric they care about. And so my buddy's making millions of dollars. All he does is he goes out there and just waste their money to get impressions. Like, that's it. Isn't that the worst thing? They got a budget of X amount of billions of dollars, and they just want to waste it. And that's like networks of dream people. Because it's sweet. No one's even clicking on these ads. This is fantastic. We're staying in our network, and that's what they want. We're taking people off, and then anyway, they don't want that. Yeah, good question. All right, so this is kind of the framework for the next two days is the funnel catcher. And I'm going to walk through this really close. And then there's a lot of stuff. This is what, inside of a company, what we're focusing on right now, and what kind of the focus is of this weekend. All right. So first thing I want to talk about, again, this is from the .com Secrets book. And so this is kind of foundational stuff. But it's important to understand it. So this is a concept that, when I first kind of understood this, it changed things for me. I was always about creating a product and selling it. And then we started learning about funnels. It was like creating a product and upselling, we selling it. And one day, I sat down. I was thinking about retail stores. You walk into a typical retail store, and I love watching the process. What's happening to me as I go through this thing? I think about how many of you guys have been inside of a GNC before? And most people are like, I hate walking into it. And I love supplements more than probably anyone, except for Anthony. Where's Anthony? And maybe Josh. There's a couple of dudes. So I love supplements. I love going to GNC. But I always avoid it. Because as they walk in, this is the lifeline they're taking their customers. You walk through the door, and a second you get in, and something pops up. Hey, can I help you with anything? And you're like, no. And they're like, OK. And then you just shut off that thing, and they walk away. And then you're nervous. And you feel, it's just this horrible experience every single time. I can't stand going to GNC. It's bad. So I watched that whole process over and over. Like, how do I feel? And why do I feel that way? And then I go to a store like The Buckle. How many of you guys go to The Buckle and shop there? The Buckle? You guys never heard of The Buckle? I thought that was a big thing. It's not a boysy thing. Anyway, The Buckle's a store, right? You guys should go to The Buckle and see the process. So I buy all my clothes at The Buckle. Everything, 100%. And the reason why is when you walk in the door initially, if you're a woman, they have great looking athletic men who come and approach you. If you're a man, they've got really cute women to come to you. Really? So you walk in, and the girl comes up to you, and she's like, wow. These are like, she's like, wow. Are you looking for a shirt or pants or something? Because I have some pants. I think would look awesome on you. Like, come over here. And she grabs your arm and pulls you, and you're like, oh, thanks. And then she's like, oh, look at me. Oh, these pants, these would look amazing on you. These are awesome. So I put them on her shirt. Come over here. And she tries to like, oh, this shirt would look good. OK. And then she's getting all this stuff. And then she's like, OK, let me get you a dresser. She puts you in there. And you're like, OK. So you're trying stuff on. She's like, come on. What's it look like? And you come out. And she's like, whoa. Those look great on you. And you're like, oh, well, thank you. I appreciate that. And then she's like, this would look even better if you had a belt. And she comes and brings a belt. And then I'm like, oh, yeah, that is look good. She's like, OK, cool. I'll get you the belt, too. And then she's like, some of the girls are really good. One girl was like, hey, we just got these new fragrances in. And she sprayed like four or five cards. Like, which one's your favorite smell? And like, oh, that one smells really good. She's like, oh, yeah, that's my favorite, too. I'm like, oh, really? Well. And like, this whole process, you know? I'm going through this sales process. And at the end, I've got like eight pairs of jeans, like 30 shirts, a couple belts, cologne, all this stuff. I'm like, man, like, and I feel amazing about just giving these guys money. Like, I was so excited, right? I'm looking at that process, how different it is, right? And so I started, yeah, there's two buckles here, boys. You should go through the process. It's really cool. Anyway, so I started looking at like my customers. Like, what is the whole process they're going through? Not just like when they come to my website, but everything from like the very first time they click on something to like the very end of my world. Like, what does that all look like? I was trying to diagram it all out. And I went through a bunch of iterations. And finally, I kind of came up with this. This is kind of, in my mind, the process that we're taking somebody through with the customer, right? Like, these are the phases they go through. And so I'm going to kind of walk through these, because each one's got some important things that are key. And again, these are key to understand because as we go to the funnel catcher stuff, when you understand these, everything else will make more sense, OK? So the first thing I look at, and I'll talk a lot about this, the first phase in my funnel is understanding traffic temperature, OK? Warm traffic versus cold traffic versus hot traffic, OK? How many of you guys have ever had a sales page or something that you sent to your own list and people bought like crazy? And you're like, this is awesome. And then you go and you buy some ads in Google or on a banner ad or something like that, and nobody buys. And you're like, but it converted like 12% over here. That's like most people's biggest problem, is that they're really good at converting to their warm market. And the reality is your warm market will buy almost anything you give them. They just will buy stuff. But you take that exact same thing you created and you take that to a cold audience, most of them they're not going to buy it, because it's different. I'll talk about that. I might jump ahead and then come back, because it's important to understand this. So you've got cold traffic, warm traffic, hot traffic. And the biggest thing is the way we speak to people down here and the way we speak to people has to be different. Let me kind of walk you through. If you've read the Doc Ong Secrets book, I learned this concept initially from a guy named Gene Schwartz on page 119 of the book. If you want to read long, just joking. This is what he said. And this is when I first got this. This was a big huge epiphany for me, because I always had created funnels and things that worked really good to my warm market, worked good to my partners, but I tried to go and advertise, and it just didn't convert. I'm like, why are these guys not buying? It doesn't make sense to me. These people are buying here. These people are buying here. Hot market's buying. Warm market's buying. But nobody's buying. My cold market. What am I doing wrong? It didn't make any sense to me. And I read this, and it was one of those aha moments for me. So Gene Schwartz said, if your prospect is aware of your product and has realized it can satisfy his desire, your headline starts with the product. If he's not aware of your product, but only the desire itself, your headline starts with the desire. If he's not yet aware of what he really seeks, but is concerned with the general problem, your headline starts with the problem and crystallizes into a specific need. And so I started thinking about that. I was thinking about this. You got cold people. You got warm people. And you got hot people. Your hot people are most aware. They know who you are. They love you. They're followers. They're true fans. You could basically put up almost anything they're going to buy. How many of you guys have I was like, hey, there's some cool supplements out there that are awesome. You guys should go buy them. How many of you guys would just run out and buy it without even like, that was it. I always thought I'd tell you. I'd say 20% of the room would just go buy it. That's your hot prospect. They trust you so much. I don't even care. Russell has cool. I'm in. Boom. I did a podcast about my number one entrepreneurial supplement. And I just said, if you're interested, message me. It's like $300. It's super expensive. I didn't tell anybody what it did or what it was about. I said, I loved it. How many of you guys listened to that podcast? How many of you guys bought afterwards? I sold 80, $8,300 packages just by saying there's this really cool supplement I like. I didn't say what it was. I didn't say anything. I said, I liked it. 80 packages, $300 a pot. That's your warm market, right? And so the problem is we're selling to our warm market. We're like, sweet, everyone loves my stuff. Then you go over to your cold market and nobody buys. You're like, why doesn't anyone buy? Because they're not aware, right? Our goal, our entire goal, and you'll see this process, our goal is to move people here. Because here we can sell people really, really easily. But the majority of people aren't here. The masses are way over here. So here's the most aware right here. Next you move backwards. The next is product aware. They know they need an auto responder, but they're trying to figure out should I use Aweber or get a response or melt. They're kind of aware. They're trying to pick what product makes the most sense. They're product aware. Then you go down. They're more solution aware. I know that I need to send emails to my customers. I know that's something that needs to happen. But that's all I know. I got only a thing else. So I'm going to speak to that person a little bit differently. And then you step back here. They're problem aware. No one's buying my crap. I don't even know what to do. I got a problem, but I don't even know what the solution is. So there's that. And down here they're unaware. They're just like, la-dee-da. They have no idea. And so this is the key to understand. And we're going to talk a lot about this this morning. Because if you grasp this, it'll give you guys the ability to take your funnels and your offers and scale them dramatically. Because the people that you're typically speaking to, if you're on Facebook right now, the pros and the cons of Facebook is I can target people who are product aware most aware. That's why it's awesome. I go in there, I want to promote to all the coaches, consultants, the authors, everyone's falling Tony Robbins, everyone's falling Russell, everyone's falling Frank. That's what most of us do. We find people that are already right here. We put up our ads, and we're making time to money off this hot market. The problem is that hot market's only this big. It's finite. It's growing, but it grows really, really slow. And so your offers, you can make a lot of money because those people are rabid buyers. But you're also limited by what you can do. If you want to scale, if you want to open up a whole world of traffic and customers and people, you have to learn how to change your offers from hot down to cold. Because at the unaware level or the problem aware, there's hundreds of millions or billions of people that could use your product. So it's understanding that, and then we're going to go through a process. But when you understand that, you speak to these people differently. So we'll come back to that. But that's the first thing I understand is that there's different types of traffic. So that's the basic number one here, is understanding there's hot traffic, there's warm traffic, there's cold traffic. And the way that I approach each of these types of traffic is going to be different. All right, so that moves now to step number two, which is our pre-framed bridge. And I want to spend a minute on pre-framing because this is the key to everything. If you get this, man, this will be worth 10 times your entire investment to be here, is the concept of framing. And it seems really, really simple. And I'm afraid that some of you guys will just move past it because it's so simple. But it's really important. So in NLP, there's a concept called framing, where here's a frame. The frame that someone enters through and they move through something, that frame will have more effect on the outcome on the next side than anything else. So let me explain that. So when I first got in this business, my first mentor was a guy named Mark Joyner. And I remember he did this tele-seminar call, Not All Clicks Are Created Equal. I'm like, what does that mean? And he got in this tele-seminar and he's like, a lot of people think that a click to your website's a click. And he's like, it's not. Not all clicks are created equal. He started talking about his pre-framing concept. He said, look, if you were to go to a website, and you see this ad that says, or a blog post that says, hey, Russell Brunson is a scam artist. I signed up for his program. He took $25,000. He never showed up. He didn't deliver. He didn't come to the event. It was a horrible experience. I hope he dies. Click here to check out his new product. OK? That's a frame someone just went through. They click on it and they see my thing. That's a frame they just went through, right? How did that frame effect what's going to happen on the next page? Is anyone going to buy? No. But they come to a frame that says, hey, Russell Brunson, I was scared. I invested $25,000. It scared the crap out of me. But after I did that, first off, I found out he really cared. He showed up at the events. He put it all in. He did everything he could to make sure that I was successful. And from that process, I went from not having any money to making a lot of money. In fact, this year we did over $1 million. You should check out his new product. Click down below. The sales page is exactly the same. The funnel they're going to is the exact same. The only difference is the frame that someone entered in when they got there. This is true not just in online sales. It's true in everything, right? It's true in phone sales. It's true in personalities and relationships. I might talk tomorrow about this, like how you do everything in life. The frame you enter something through, your personality, how you enter anything, any situation in life, that frame will completely change the outcome. So it's a big deal. But the first thing is that pre-frame, right? Now every single step in this process is in and of itself a pre-frame, OK? It's a frame for the next thing. That's kind of a key to understand as well. The front end product is a pre-frame for the upsell. The upsell is a pre-frame for my high ticket thing. So this framing concept is not just on step two. It's happening in every single step along the way. And we see in the funnel hacker concept, the same thing like a funnel catcher, everything we're doing are pre-frames. So that's key to understand. So the pre-frame bridge I take someone through is going to be different depending on what type of traffic they're coming from, right? So if this traffic is hot and they love me and they think on the greatest thing in the world, my pre-frame bridge typically is really, really small. I can send an email and that can be my pre-frame. I can say, this is something that's freaking awesome. Go buy it. That can be my pre-frame. It can be really, really short because those people are so warm they don't need a lot. It's something to get them in motion and moving forward. With your warm audience, people typically don't have a relationship with you, but they have a relationship with somebody. And so if I did a webinar with, for example, we did a webinar with Siobhan a little while ago. And so she's got a warm audience that love her, trust her. So she did a webinar with us. She was a warm audience. And she said, hey, Russell's webinar is awesome. You guys should go check that. Her email to her list was a pre-frame. They came to that pre-frame and it was an email. And that's what warmed them up to then come and be on the webinar and participate and to buy. So that's the pre-frame bridge. With cold traffic, which is going to be the majority of what we talk about today and tomorrow, typically the pre-frame bridge is going to be longer. Because a lot of times, especially, let's say I'm coming to someone. I'm approaching them down here where they're problem aware. Let's say, for example, let's say you're in the weight loss space. We flew out last month and spent time with Dave Asprey. And his whole mission, his whole message is you need to eat high fat, butter in your coffee, all that kind of stuff. The diets have 80% of your calories need to come from fat. Now that's something that's over here. You've got to be really aware to understand why that's good. If I come down to these people over here, I'm like, hey, this is the deal. You're fat because someone's problem aware. I'm problem. I'm overweight. I'm miserable. If I came to them, I'm like, hey, this is the secret. If you want to lose some weight, you've got to get up to your calories. 80% of your calories need to come from fat. Are you kidding me? I'm a big fat guy. There's no way I'm going to eat more fat. It doesn't make sense. But they're problem aware. They understand they're overweight. So I've got to come to them at that spot. And I need to give them a pre-frame bridge to help them understand that. One of the products that we got brought on to do the funnels stuff for is a new product that puts your body in ketosis, which is kind of a similar thing we're trying to do over here. So my thing is, how do I get this out to the masses? I've got to figure out something that gets them at their problems. So the ad that we're writing for, something like these weird salts force your body to eat its own fat. That's this unique headline. Someone's out there and they're feeling overweight. And they're like, this salt's going to make my body eat its own fat. Does that make you curious? I've got to click on that. So you click on that. Then it'll take you over to this problem where you're thinking, hey, if you've been overweight and you've tried this diet and this diet and this diet, there's something that's kind of interesting. There's these new type of salts. When you take them, it actually causes your body to eat its own fat. Can you imagine that? And it talks about this process. It doesn't say ketosis. It doesn't say high fat diet. It doesn't say all these things. It meets them where they're at and explains this cool thing that can do it. And now in here, I'm transitioning them from being a problem aware up to solution aware. Now they understand this is why a high fat diet's important. And I transitioned to here and then to here. So I'm using this pre-frame to meet them right where they're at, grab them, hold their hand, and teach them, and educate them, and get them to be warmed up. Does that make sense? And I'm going to show you guys 100 examples of this today. But that's kind of the concept. So that's what these pre-frame bridges are. The whole goal of them is to grab some more of their at and get them prepared for the next message. And again, it's going to be different based on if they're warm traffic, cold traffic, hot traffic, and hot traffic. All right. OK. So that's phase number two. Now phase number three is after that traffic was people have come over to me, my next goal is I've got to figure out of all these people that came to my thing, which people are so much pain that they're willing to trade me their email address in exchange for more information and more whatever. I've got to figure out who are my subscribers. Because most people will never subscribe to your email list. And if they won't subscribe to your email list, they're probably not going to buy anything. And so I want to find out as quick as possible who's willing to actually give me their email address. So my next phase in here is my opt-in form, my squeeze page, my whatever. And there's a ton of different ways we can do that. And we'll talk about those. But somewhere where I'm getting their email address. So that's the next step. I get their email address. Now I know of all the traffic I set here, these are the people who are in so much pain and they're willing to exchange something for more. They're giving me their email address. That's a level of commitment. One thing you'll notice through this process that's kind of interesting, I served a mission for two years. And so I was out knocking on doors every single day in New Jersey in the swell, turning heat with a shirt and tie on. And it was awesome. And so I was doing that. And what's interesting is after talking to thousands of people, get the door slammed in your face more times than you would ever believe, it was interesting. I started seeing that if we could get people to make little tiny commitments, initially, then I could give them a bigger commitment. Like people who make small commitments will make bigger commitments. People who won't make the small commitment will never make the big commitment. So I get in the door, I knock on the door, and I've got one second for them to give me a commitment. If they make a commitment, then I can start talking. So I'd ask them a quick question. I saw right here you got some toys on the ground. Like, do you have some kids? Like, oh, yeah, I got three kids. Boom, they just made a commitment. They just talked to me, right? So I made a little micro commitment. I'm saying, awesome. One of the favorite things about the church we're out talking about is that we believe families can be forever. And they're like, oh, do you believe families can be forever? And they're like, yeah. Boom, I made another commitment. I asked these questions. And I'm trying to get in. And then I asked them another question. And then I'm trying to make these little commitments. And I might say, hey, I need you to read this verse. I'm going to give you this book. I want you to read this verse. I'm going to come back tomorrow. And we'll talk about it. I'm like, OK. So I give them the book. I leave. I come back next day. If they read a verse, then I know that next I can teach a lesson. And I can progress someone. If I come back, I didn't have a chance to read it. I know instantly that person's not someone who will make or keep commitments. So I saw that over and over. And as two years of getting the door slammed in my face, I saw that process dozens and dozens of times. It's the exact same thing here. I'm trying to get people to make commitments. Every single step along this way. And you'll see some really cool, when we talk about some of the pre-framed versions, some of the quizzes, some of the cool things where I'm getting these little micro commitments to happen. If I can cause these little micro commitments, it's amazing the pre-frame they cause on the next side. One of the guys who's in our one of our mastermind groups, he had a webinar. He was driving traffic webinars, making a bunch of money. And then he thought, I'm going to put a quiz in front of the webinar. So he just took a quiz. Everything else didn't change at all. So he put this quiz out there. And the quiz is like, asked five or six random questions. And he said, yes. Then took him to the webinar page. Nothing else changed at all. Webinar was the same. Registration, everything else was the same. All he did is he put this thing to qualify subscribers in front, to get them doing something, to get them making little, tiny commitments. Went through that process. And the webinar stats, he did two and a half times as many cells on the webinar, just by making some of these little micro commitments, by doing little. It's just crazy. It's so weird to happen. So this whole process, I'm trying to get people to make little commitments. So click on this ad. Read this article. Give me your email address. I'm making these commitments. Because if they make the little ones, people make little commitments and make big commitments. And I want to find out who those people are quick. Because I want to weed out all the rest of the people. Those who won't make commitments, I don't have time to focus on them. I don't want to worry about them. I only want to work with people who make and keep commitments. So that's the phase number four. Now, after they've given me their email address, they come into my world. Now, I want to find out the next step is not only who will make commitments, but of all the people that have given me their email address, how many of those people are actually buyers? I need to know as quick as possible who my buyers are. I learned from Dan Kennedy when I first got started in this business. He said, you have to understand that a buyer is a buyer is a buyer. Somebody who buys once will continue to buy until you screw up the relationship. That's how it works. And so I want to find out as quick as possible who are my buyers. Because if I know who my buyers are, I can work with them different. I can do different things. I'll spend more money to market to them. That's why you guys have seen my book funnel. Why did I get my book for free? You want to find out instantly who are my buyers? Because if they're willing to put in their credit card for a free book, now they've qualified themselves. Now I know they're buyers. Now I might call them on the phone. I might send them a sales letter in the mail. I might market to them differently. But I've got to find out who my buyers are as quick as possible. So step number four, qualify my buyers. Then identify, survey number five is now, not only do I want to know my buyers, who are my hyperactive buyers. How many of you guys in this room are hyperactive buyers? You're in this room. Just saying. This might be time for us to have an intervention. And there's nothing wrong with that. I'm sure some of you guys have heard me tell this story before, but I am definitely hyperactive buyer. When I get into something, I get really, really into something. Like I think one time Anthony, I had a, I don't know what Anthony's at. Over there. We had a call, and we're just talking about supplements. And he just mentions 20 supplements he takes. And I'm like, right now, as fast as I could. And while we're on the phone, I'm like, buying him, buying him, buying him. Because I'm like, those all sound amazing. I want all of them right now. I don't want to wait. And I'm rush shipping. Amazon Prime's got two days. I'm like, I can't wait for two days. Like I got to do the overnight. And I'm like, if there was an option that they'd just drop me off in an hour, I would have done that. Because I'm just like, I need these in my body. We went bowling with our company a couple years ago. And I love bowling. It's my third favorite sport. And behind wrestling, then jujitsu and bowling. Actually, it was bowling number two. And then jujitsu bumped it out recently. Anyway, so we're bowling with our team. And I'm like, I'm not an amazing bowler. But I can hold my own, right? I can break 200 every once in a while. So we're bowling. And I beat everybody on the team. So for one guy, beats me. And of course, he knows how competitive I am. He's got to make fun of me for the rest of the night. And I'm just like, in so much pain, right? I'm a buyer in pain. So I go home that night. And I'm just like, my wife's asleep. I'm sitting there just angry. And I'm like, I can't believe you beat me. Like, I got to figure this out. So I'm searching online, like bowling, like trying to figure out, what did I do wrong? Was I stepping too far? Whatever. I'm like, crap, I can't figure it out from all this free stuff. I need to buy something. It'll make me feel better. I went and I bought some new bowling shoes. I was like, how shoes suck? Like, obviously, that's why I'm slipping. Right? My own shoes, I can take care of the environment or the situation. I had my own ball, but I found out there's a strike ball and a spare ball. I'm like, well, crap, I had the spare ball. I would have beat him. So I bought two new balls. I got one of the wrist guards because by the third game, my hook was gone because whatever. So I bought the wrist guard. And I went through the night. I spent tons of money. I bought some training DVDs. I bought just as much bowling stuff as I could that night because I was in pain and I needed to solve that problem. And then the morning I woke up and I forgot about it. And a week later, all this bowling stuff starts coming, my wife's pissed. And I'm like, oh, yeah. I was in pain. I was a buyer in heat. And so I want to figure out. You've got to figure out who your buyers in heat are. Because this is the deal. When somebody's in that pain and they want to get out of it, they're not just coming to your website, as much as you may think, that you're the only person in the world. They go to your website, and they go to your competitors, and they start buying as much stuff as out there. Because they want to get their problem solved. And so if you're not willing to give it to them, they're going to go somewhere else for it. And I want to find out who those people are. Who is serious about this? Not just like I'm dabbling, but I'm into it. And so that's why we have our upsells. That's why we have other things in place. Because I want to identify exactly who my hyperactive buyers are. Because again, I'm going to treat those people different. Somebody spends $1,000 in upsells buying my product. We can market those people different. We're going to call them differently. We're going to do different things with them. So that's the next step. Identify your hyperactive buyers. Now that, you guys know this process right here, right? From here, step five, this is all happening in one session, in the funnel. They see and add, they click on it, they read something, they subscribe, they buy, they upsell, and boom. That's the transaction. So that's all happening point of sale. If we were at the buckle, this is everything that ladies sold me at the cart. Now I got all my stuff cool. Now the problem with most people is they do all that. And then they're like, all right, well, we're good. And I start thinking about what's the next step? Where do we go from here? And this is where we start transitioning to the next step. After someone's bought and they've gone through this good experience, now our next step in the process is to take that relationship that we've started. And how do we ascend that? How do we take it somewhere different? Someone bought my book. That's awesome. I'm really, really excited. But my goal for someone that isn't just to buy the book. My goal is for them to come here and be in this room right here. And from this room, it's awesome. I love you guys in this room. But I want all of you guys in the inner circle. How many of you guys were in the inner circle last two days? Was it freaking amazing or what? Yeah. I want you guys in that group. I'm going to keep trying to send you guys and give you as much value as possible because you should be in that group. That's my goal is I'm trying to age and ascend this relationship. What can I do? How much more value can I provide? What can I do cool to get you and get other people to ascend up to those higher levels? That's the goal. And typically, if you want to get people to buy more expensive things, you can't always do it in the same selling environment. It's really hard to sell a $25,000 thing on an order form. Anyone ever gone to a website, saw an order button, clicked on it, 25 grand and filled out the credit card and clicked submit? Someone in here has. I know someone has. It's hard. I've tried to sell. I think the highest I've ever sold just from an order form is $5,000. I was like, I can't believe people bought. Not many people bought, but we sold some. I was like, dang, I tried 10 one time and nobody bought. I was like, OK. So to get people to spend more money, you have to get them out of their comfort zone. You have to change the, you have to break the pattern, get them out of where they're at. Because most people, in fact, Noah was telling me the story yesterday. When he was moving his house, he had his corner desk, he's got his computers, he's got affirmations, this is his world. He said that he moved his entire house and didn't touch that because he didn't want to screw with his, this is his domain. He owns this. This is like, he has 100% control here, right? And he moved entire house out and finally was like, OK, I'll break this down. He took that out last, right? And that's what you're dealing with with people, right? They're at their home, in their computer. They've got like, you know, mine, I've got my monitors wrapping me. I've got all this stuff. I've got my music. I've got like, this is, like, I own this spot. I control it, right? And so for you to come and like, try to like, penetrate that and try to get somebody out of that and to sell something, it's really, really hard. Like, so if I want to get somebody, I need to change the selling environment. I got to take them away from their computer. I got to get them on the phone. I got to take them away and get them on a webinar. I got to take them away, get them to a live. I got to change that process somehow because when they're sitting there protected in their spot, it's hard to, I mean, it's just like you're breaking a fortress, right? So we got to change that experience. And so that's kind of the last phase here, is changing the selling environment. So those are the core phases of a funnel. I know somebody hasn't gone through that before. I want you to understand that because this will help, again, it's a good framework for everything else we'll be covering today. All right, so here's the spectrum again. So something that you think about is this is the goal of all my marketing, right? To move someone from cold to warm to hot. Okay, in all aspects of life, I would say. I think I'm one of my wives the first time, right? I met her, I'm like, dang, she is cute, right? But she's the cold prospect. She, problem aware. She knew that she wanted someone she knows me. So I got to warm her up to the idea, right? And make her solution aware and then product aware and we're moving people through this process, right? Indating it happens, with employees it happens, like any aspect of your life, like that's what's happening, right? So all of my marketing, like that's the goal is to get somebody to move. And prospects, people, your customers, whatever you want to call them will come into your business and they'll migrate through it. Sometimes people are, ha, they're excited, but then that's why they might cool off and they become warm and they might even become cold again. So it's always a constant thing of like, how do I get people to the hot side and keep them there, right? So everything we're doing in business is kind of doing that thing, right? So again, we talked about earlier in internet marketing, there's kind of three core funnels, right? We've got our tripwire funnel, we've got our webinar funnel and our high ticket funnel, right? Now think about this, like the goal of the tripwire is to convert a cold customer, right? And then we move them out to webinar where they're warmer and then a high ticket where they're hot, right? Like that's how I always thought business was, like I always thought like, this is the path you have to take everybody through, which is true, right? This works. But what's interesting is that actually the first time I really thought about this, because I looked at my business and my business is all about this. Like I had free plus shipping offers, I had low ticket offer, bunch of front end things that I only focus on cold traffic when we ascended people up. I started working with Liz and I remember she was like, okay, Russell, I'm gonna build a tripwire, then a webinar and then a high ticket. And I was like, I remember my first call with her, I'm like, I don't think, a year personality, I think we'll do better on a webinar. Like I think we should just cut and go straight to webinars. She's like, but how many cold leads to a send up and all stuff? I'm like, and I started thinking more about this. And I realized that while this is true, right? You do take somebody through from cold to warm to hot in a funnel like this. You also, you kind of do it at every step in the funnel as well, okay? So for example, we drive cold traffic to all three of these pages and all three of them convert. But to get them to convert, the sales process is a little bit different, to get them from cold to warm to hot, okay? So down here on my low ticket, it's easy to get somebody to get warmed up, right? I'm like, hey, do you wanna learn how to make money online? Here's a free book. The sales process is simple, right? It's not that hard and the price barrier is really low, so it's not that hard, okay? On a webinar, some of my come and registered for a webinar. And the sales process is different though, because they didn't already buy some for me, they don't know anything about me. So I gotta take them through a webinar, right? A 90 minute presentation. And the goal of my webinar, like look at the perfect webinar script, one of the major goals of that is to convert someone from cold to warm to hot, right? Like how many guys got on the funnel hacks webinar and never knew who I was before that webinar? That's awesome. How many guys by the end gave me a whole bunch of money and then upgraded and now you're here? Okay, the same amount of people, right? Because that process of going from cold to warm to hot, and same thing with high ticket. Liz had never heard of me, Liz had never bought from me. She came and saw the page, watched the video, and then she showed her high ticket program. And tons of people went and just watched the video and then they joined the program because, but the process is different, right? It's not a sales letter, it's only $25,000 a thing. It's not a webinar, it's a different process. So for example, if I'm selling a low ticket product, the script is Star Store solution, the goal of that script is to get some from cold to warm to hot, okay? Think of the perfect webinar, the goal of that is to get some from cold to warm to hot. And then goal of the two step, this is our call center script, is to go from cold to warm to hot, okay? And so each of those funnels, like in and of itself, that's the goal of it, is to get someone cold to warm to hot. So you can drive traffic to all of them when you understand that's how it works, okay? And so people always ask me, well Russell, which funnel should I do first? Should I do a trip wire first? Should I do webinar first? Should I do high ticket first? Okay, so how many of you guys already have your front end funnel done? You know what it is. Cool, how many of you guys don't yet? You're kind of in between. It's about half and half, I'd say. So when you're thinking through this, I was thinking about this the other day, that how we sell in each is a little bit different, right? The very first one is either driven by a sales letter, like text copy, or a video of me basically giving a sales letter, right? So it's sold that way. So if you're someone who's comfortable either writing or selling through video, that's probably the best one for you to start with, okay? In fact, how many of you guys feel like that's your boat? I can really write a sales letter or I can pitch something like a free plus shipping thing. How many of you guys feel like that's where you're comfortable selling something? Okay, cool. So that's probably the front end funnel you should focus on. Okay, next one is a webinar, right? Now this one, the sales process is different. This is typically someone who's got a ton of energy, a ton of excitement in there, they're captivating an audience for 90 minutes, and then selling something at the end. How many of you guys feel like that's my selling platform, like that one fires me up and I want to sell that way all the time? Okay, about a third there, okay? So if that's like your style of selling, then that's awesome, right? Now how many of you guys like, I just want to go on the phone with someone, if they pick up the phone they're giving me money. How many of you guys feel like that? Okay, so about a third, so we got a third, a third, a third. And so for you, I would say you can start with any funnel, I don't care which one it is, but think about your style, okay? For me, if I had to get on the phone with someone and try to sell them on the phone, nothing on earth scares me more. Like it freaks me out so bad. In fact, sometimes Robbie, Randy, or Derek will be like, hey, can you jump on the phone and this person has a couple of questions before they sign up. I'm like, I will lose the sale for you every time. And they done it twice and both times I lost the cell for him. And so we don't do anymore. Like they know don't put Russell on the phone because he sucks at phone sales more than anyone on earth. Like it scares me so bad, yeah. Some people do, what I'm saying is eventually you're going to want all of them. But at first, you don't want to do all of them, right? You got to find out what you're most comfortable with. For me to do a high ticket thing, I knew that I would never go on the phone. So until I had people in place who that was their superpower and they could get on the phone, they could close sales, I wouldn't do that one. Now that I have it, I could do it, right? If you're like a webinar person and you suck on webinars, like you probably shouldn't do a webinar funnel first, right? And this is kind of interesting. I hope Jason and I are teasing Mike. Jason's going on webinars, doing webinars really, really good. But then Joel came, where's Joel at? Joel right here, because they're like, hey, I'm pretty good at webinars. Jason, how do you have a webinar? And Jason's like, sure, man. So Joel does his webinar and like three times I may sell Jason in. Jason's like, sweet, I'm never doing a webinar again. And Joel's been crushing webinars for like a hundred and a month, and I'm just crushing over and over and over again. Because Joel's lost some webinars, right? So if you suck at webinars, don't do it. Either find someone to do that webinar for you. Like I found someone to do my high ticket sales or don't start there, right? With the front end, if you feel good on video or writing a sale, whatever, again, whatever, these three selling styles is different. That's the core thing I'm trying to get. There are different ways to sell. So you got to figure out for you, like which one you're most comfortable with that you like, that's where you start at. And that's where you initially get people in. And then from there, you can go and you can start building out the other ones, building out a team, finding people to do other pieces. But initially figure out like what you're most comfortable with, you're selling style and starting there. Yeah, yeah. So her comment was, some of my training paths I talked about, like a lot of times I'll start with the back end first. And that's more on the product development side. Because on the product development side, like I might do a big event, record it, and then that might become all the stuff for the tripwire, right? Or it might become what I sell on the webinar, or it might become what I sell on the high ticket. So from a product creation standpoint, 100% grief there. But then how do I sell that product? There's different selling styles, right? So that's kind of what I'm saying right here. So how many guys again, how many guys are tripwire people? How many guys are webinar people? How many guys are high ticket people? How many guys feel comfortable with two things? How many people could go to three things? There are a couple super humans here. All right, I personally feel really good at tripwire and webinar. Like I feel like I can sell anything any day, all day long, but don't get me on the phone, okay? So that's kind of what I want you guys kind of thinking through. All right, so my question for you guys then is what is going to be your very first break-even funnel? Because that's the goal, right? That's the next, the 10 weeks we're going to go on with you guys starting on Monday is building out a break-even funnel. Like that's the thing. So you got to figure out which one of the three funnels is the one you want to focus on. Yes, I can. In fact, the next slide happens to be the break-even funnel. Yeah. All right, so this is the goal, right? This is the goal of the 10 week course. This is the goal of every funnel we create initially is I want to break-even funnel, okay? And so this is what a break-even funnel is. Basically, I've got traffic coming into a funnel. And if this funnel breaks even, so I put a dollar in Facebook ads and I make a dollar, I won. It's a million dollar a year business. Okay, that's the goal. The goal is not to build a webinar or a funnel or whatever that makes me, $300,000 a month. Like that's cool and sometimes that happens. It's really exciting. But that's not the goal. All you need is my goal in business is to get customers, right? When I have customers, now I can go take them from cold to warm to hot and these people buy anything I tell them in the future, right? That's the goal. Okay, and I'm going to talk about that here before lunch, some of the things we do to get people hot and get them warm and get in there. But that's my goal is to get people so I can build a bond, build a relationship so they'll buy everything I tell them to do, right? So my initial goal is not to tell them everything. My initial goal is to get that customer in, right? But a customer can cost a lot of money, right? Like when Jason, for your webinar, how much do you spend on average per webinar registered? So $3.60 he pays for someone to register for his webinar, right? Now that's awesome. That brings somebody into your world. But you got to have something to break you, to cover those costs, right? So every thousand people is costing $3,600. For a hundred thousand people it's costing, it gets expensive. And so your first funnel is just, the whole goal of that is just to break even. So every single customer I get is, I'm getting customers for free. Because if you build a business where you get customers for free, then everything else you sell them afterwards is 100% profit. You keep all of that. And that's the business talking about. So you create a break even funnel, you drive traffic into this, you spend a dollar, you break even, and then at the bottom of this pops out a customer. Oh, I got a customer now. And if I got that customer for free, that's a win. Okay, that's a million dollar your business. If I have a funnel, it can get me customers for free. And sometimes we get a funnel that doesn't break even, but it loses money. Okay, it loses money. Some businesses, that's how they operate. Some businesses, in fact, you guys right here, what's in your funnel, what's your break even point typically? How long does it take till you get to break even? So six months. So they lose money on the break even funnel. And after six months of selling these things right here, then they break even, and then they make money. They're like 30, 40 million bucks a year, big deal, right? But they're able to go, they understand they can lose money during this initial funnel and still build a huge, huge, huge business, right? And so you don't have to break even immediately. Now for me, not breaking even immediately stresses me out so bad that I can't do it. How many guys are in a similar boat? Okay, when we launched NuraSell, the second funnel we did was like a free plus shipping thing. And it took us 45 days to break even. And like, oh, it's like, it made me, like, my risk tolerance can't handle that. Like it stresses me out. Some people are like, oh, it's cool. And in fact, I got friends who do five or 6,000 bottles a day on free plus shipping. Takes them 90 days to break even, and they get lenders to lend them money for that time. And I'm just like, man, that stresses me out so bad. But that's like their whole business model, right? So if you're comfortable with that, that's good. But I'm not. So my goal is I have to at least break even. So if I can break even, I'm like ecstatic. Now sometimes we build a funnel that doesn't break even. Sometimes we build a funnel that's profitable. How many of you guys have a funnel right now that's profitable on the front end? Couple people? What's it feel like? Yeah. If you can spend $1 an hour and add and make $2 or $3 or $5 or $10, that's amazing. So obviously, my goal for you guys is to get better than a break even. But if you have a break even, you should be celebrating. That's a big deal. That's an exciting thing. Because now you're getting customers for free. Customers come in. And now all the other stuff we want to offer these guys in the future is just pure cash. A good example of this. Liz Benia. I wish she was here. She'd be fun telling you guys a story. She voxed me last night. And she did nothing but a webinar for almost a year. I think they did $7,800 just from the webinar. That's the one funnel she had. Now she's like, OK, now I want to do a back end thing. So we helped to structure a back end funnel. And she sent an email to a little tiny portion of her list because she didn't know how to sell or anything. And she got a couple of sales. And then she stopped for two months because she just was waiting for whatever reason. And then two days ago, she turned her high ticket funnel back on. She emailed the tiny portion of Liz. She got 17 applications. First two people they called, they closed for, I think, 10 or 12 grand a piece. And then yesterday she called. And the third person they called, they sold $25,000. And guess what their costs were for those customers? Zero. Because the webinar already paid for those people months and months and months ago. So every single penny of that money, she keeps 100%. That's the goal with this entire business, you guys. Build the funnel breaks even so that tomorrow or a month now or whenever it is, you decide to open up a continuity program, a high ticket, you print out something else's offer. You make up a new idea for a product or whatever it is, when you sell to those people, 100% of the money comes back to you. And that's where the business wins. So this is our goal with creating a break even funnel. In fact, you actually get this all tattooed to your arm or whatever it is. Because if you get that, it's off to the races. Now this is what it feels like when you have a successful break even funnel. Anyway, I've used this on three presentations. It's like my favorite picture in the world, favorite gift in the world. Unlimited free money. All right. Okay, so typically if you guys want a good break even funnel, it's got to be a cold traffic break even funnel. Because it's nice to have a break even funnel that works to Tony Robbins successful coaches. But that world is like this big. Okay, so if I want something that I can scale, that I can make a lot more money, we've got to figure out how to make it a cold offer. Now, something to talk about for the next little bit here is, how do we make this offer a cold offer? Because this cold offer, then I can go to the masses and it opens up my world infinitely. Whereas if I keep it warm, I can make a lot of money off that, but you're stuck in this little bubble. It's hard to grow beyond that. It's hard to get more traffic. It's hard to get bigger. That's why a lot of people struggle when they try to go off Facebook because they're so used to advertising this little warm market who understands them. And Facebook kicks them off. They're like, where do I get more warm people? It's hard. Okay, it's hard to go find an email issue around with author, speaker, consultants. It's hard. It's hard to go find anything consistent where I can drive those per... Like that's why Facebook was so great for a while. It's still good, but it was great for a while where I could find exactly who I wanted and get my message in front of them. And there's not many platforms you can still do that with. And Facebook says it's getting harder and harder. So if I want to really grow and scale my business, I got to have a funnel, a cold funnel that becomes my break-even funnel. Okay? All right. So we'll walk through. There's a couple of steps you have to go to have a successful cold traffic break-even funnel. And this is number one. This is going to be the exercise we're going to do. We're going to spend probably five minutes or so on this. Because this is the key. When you guys can get this, it's going to be awesome. So what we're going to do is understand that the way you communicate with people at each level is the secret. Okay? A lot of people are like, how do you convert to cold traffic, Russell? And it's 100% your language, like how you're speaking, right? Excuse me. So for example, for a hot traffic, this is one of our old offers that we made a lot of money with. We had an offer that was like how to build a list and make a whole bunch of money, right? So, warm people know what a list is, right? Cold people have no idea. This is kind of the litmus test to know if your offer is going to work to cold traffic. So imagine today at lunch, you go down to the Boise Mall, you walk into the food courts, all these people hustling and bustling, and you're watching all these people go crazy, and then you pull out of a chair, you stand up on it, and you yell from the top of your lungs, hey, who here wants to learn how to build a huge email list? How many people think the food court would raise their hand? Maybe one? Probably none. They're looking like, what is he talking about? Is he crazy? So you have Tourette's, like what's he yelling about, right? They have no idea. So that's the test. So we had a warm traffic offer that was let me show you how to build a list, right? So we're good to my warm market. Then I started going to cold traffic. I was driving ads from Newsmax and from Republican sites and from blogs and all these different things. I'm like, hey, build a list, build a list, and nobody's clicking. Like, come on guys, this is the secret sauce. Like, if you have a list, you're rich. Like, don't you get it? And they're like, no. And so we made a new offer that said, free money making website. Can you just now imagine I walk to the food court, lock in all the people, just pull out my chair, stand on it, and I'm like, hey, who wants a free money making website? How many people in the food court would be like, I'm in, right? That's how you know if your offer is going to work to cold traffic, okay? So what are we going to do right now with this exercise and how John turned the music on while we do it for about five minutes or so? It's going to take whatever your offer is right now, okay? Because I'm guessing that 90% of you guys are selling it to people who are most aware, right? So I want you to write your most aware headline or whatever you want to call it. And then I want you to write one that's a product aware, solution aware, problem aware, and unaware. And the unaware, again, the test is going to be if I stand up in the food court and I scream on top of my lungs, will the majority of people want to raise their hand. That's how we know if it works over here, okay? So if this was me right now and if I was doing this for ClickFunnels, let's say, right? My headline over here, my most aware headline would be like, who wants to have marketing automation software that builds out emails or builds out funnels and sends out emails? Okay, the geeks in the room are like, yeah, I want that. They're the most aware, right? So I'm a product aware. I'm going to say, hey, you sick of lead pages? They freaking suck, right? You can't even move things around. How do you use ClickFunnels? You can actually move things around on your page. Oh, sweet, I'm in, right? So that's my product aware. Solution aware. Are you sick and tired of trying to build websites and hiring designers and getting people to do this stuff? It's so frustrating, right? I've got a solution that's going to make this really, really easy. It's called ClickFunnels. Yeah, solution aware. Problem, yeah, then problem aware would be the thing of how many of you guys would like more leads for your business, right? They have a problem, like they have no customers. How many of you guys need customers? How many of you guys need leads? How many of you guys cool software ClickFunnels can get you guys leads and get you guys customers? And then down here, my unaware would be like, who wants to get rich on the internet? You need a funnel. I don't know. Free money making website, probably my offer again.
