All right. So was that a good exercise to get the process started? That's not the end, you guys. It's something you got to focus on. There was a book I read when I first got into network marketing like 10 years ago from a lady named Kim Claver. She was actually at the Funnel Hacks event, which was kind of fun. And in her book, she talked about why most people suck at selling network marketing stuff. Do you know the number one reason why most people are really, well, not number one, but one of the many reasons why most people are really bad at it is concepts. She said that people, they do what they call techno babble, right? Where they come in and they're like, hey, I got this new company and we got a $16 billion your company and our technology is this and blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, and everyone's like, what crap is he talking about, right? You're mostly approaching people that are most aware, right? And that's why most people fail. It's language patterns. It's a big deal, right? Techno babble is what's killing most of you guys. My guess is if your webinar is failing, 90% of it might be techno babble. You're saying all this crap that nobody understands. You gotta remember, if you watch my webinars or my trainings or most of my stuff, you'll probably notice that I talk to people way down to this side for the most time, right? And if I'm gonna say something like funnels to people up here, I define it ahead of time. My funnel hacks webinar, funnels is, I'm very aware that funnels is the most aware term, right? Most people don't know what that is. So when I start the webinar, I'm bringing, I'm starting down here and I talk about how many of you guys know what a funnel is and like 70% do and 30% don't. I'm like, okay, let me define what a funnel is. You guys heard about a website, right? Everyone knows what a website is and I talk about that and I bridge the gap between, it's called bridge page. It bridges the gap between from a website to a funnel and that brings people in. Now I can talk about funnels all I want, but I don't just lead with that because most people are sitting there half the webinar like, what is a funnel? I'm so confused with what he's even talking about, right? And so think about that in all your communication. My guess is for most of you guys to be more successful, you've got to start dumbing down your language patterns, okay? It sounds bad, but it's the key. If you speak to people closer on this side, they'll be more important. They'll understand you more. And so anyway, there's a lot of reasons to understand that, but it's the key. All right, so that's definitely one is understanding language patterns. It is such a big deal. I wish I could spend like a day or two days just on this, but it's the key. It's how we open up to the masses and you'll see more and more of that as we keep digging deeper into this. Step number two, one of the big problems I have with this, or a lot of problems that complaints people always have is like, well Russell, as we start getting into like the things we're doing is that they're upset because they're so focused on their brand. Or like, I've got my brand, I want my brand to be awesome and some of these pages and things you're telling me to do aren't very brandish, they don't look awesome. And what I want to understand is that there's a point in a time for branding and there's a point in a time for conversions. This for example is our launch funnel. How many of you guys have seen this launch funnel? No, you're not opening my email. So this is the launch funnel. A couple of things about this page looks cool, right? Who am I speaking to? Warmer cold traffic here. Hot traffic. Three of my three secret funnels. If I'm in the food court, who wants to see my three secret funnels? No one, right? I'm very aware that this is a warm traffic offer. I structure that way. With that I've got logos, I'm going to do branding, a bunch of cool stuff. So this is a hot traffic offer. How many of you guys think this is hot or cold? This is cold, it's ugly, right? How many of you guys like, I don't want any of my customers ever seen that page, it's embarrassing. That does not identify my brand, it's not who I am, I just hate it, there's no logos, there's no flowers, right? So I'm just thinking that right now. With cold traffic this is like a magnet. This will like suck people in. So a key that I need you guys to understand is that in this whole lifeline of your customers, you guys probably can see this over there, right? Everybody kind of see that? So you're driving people back in. If you want to get people initially at the cold level, back here we are not doing branding. So branding equals no. This is for conversions. Because I want to get as many people in the front doors as possible. After they have come in this door and now there are people on my list, I own that traffic, I can do things. Now we transition over to things that look good, that have logos that are branding. This is what gets your hot people to keep on buying over and over and over and over again is your brand. But your brand provides zero value initially getting people into your funnel. In fact, it's a negative deterioration. If I threw a ClickFunnels logo on this page, it would drop my conversions dramatically. Does that make sense? So when I'm going cold, you got to stop worrying about yourself because obviously I want our logo because we feel good about it. Stop thinking about yourself, think about what's going to get people to come into your world. Stacy? So that's a good question. Her question is, when do we go from ugly to branded? And a lot of times it's not initially during the first thing because if I go from that page to this page, it's such incongruity. Like, whoa, am I in the right spot? So usually we'll keep them similar. But typically one of the things we do a lot, okay, so let's say this is, we'll call this one the fugly page. So there it is. So it's ugly. And then in here what we'll do is we'll create something called a bridge page. And then here we'll take somebody over here to our branded thing. And so these two look very similar. And the goal of this is basically like, for example, work from home, realistic income for the average Joe, right? How many of you guys want to work from home? Okay, I got people to raise their hand at the food court. They're coming in. They gave me the email address. And the next step, the bridge page, what my job here is to bridge that gap between warm and cold, right? So the other version we have this page says, so like, send emails, make money, right? So send emails, make money. Then they come right here and I'll say, hey, thanks so much for subscribing for more information about sending money or sending emails, making money, right? Excuse me, the big secret in our marketing is you have to send out emails of people that want to buy your stuff. So what we do is we build what we call a list. And this list is going to be, you know, all these people and imagine you send an email and I kind of explain to them what, I'm bridging the gap between this cold to this warm concept of a list, right? So I explain that here in this video here. And then at the end I say, hey, so if you want, I made a video that's going to show you more information about my list building website and how you can get started. So then from there, I push them over to, like, let's say it's list hacking, which is not my list building website. So now they've bridged that gap, they come over here and it makes more sense. Does that kind of, does that help? You do anything. So this page right here, one of my favorite people online is named Dagan Smith. So he did a six year split test on landing pages trying to figure out the highest converting thing. And this was the result after six years. So far it's almost impossible for me to beat this in most markets. And one thing that he talked about was kind of cool. He said, with cold traffic, these people have no idea who you are. They clicked on some random ad and like, most people are scared. He called it the deer and headlights syndrome, right? He says, like, the deer's coming out from behind the tree, it sees the thing, it's coming closer and closer and any little thing happens, it freaks it out and it runs off. He did, I had a video there, right? The video comes in like, it freaks them out and they leave. We did privacy sales, which typically privacy sales on order form increase conversion, but on this page, decreased conversion. So many different things. And you look at this, it's just like, it's so simple, it's so easy. I'm asking for email address, I'm asking for anything. It's just saying, hey, how did you hear about us? Oh, TV, Dan Kennedy, email newsletter, other. Click on a button and now, boom, they just made a commitment. They clicked and it moves them in. We'll talk more about this page on other pages after lunch. But that's kind of the concept is it's this whole deer and headlights. Cold traffic is coming, they're nervous. They've been scammed in the past, they're scared. All these things happen, all these conversations happening and the more things you have that you're hitting them with, the more likely they are just to leave. And so this page is all about just simple, they come, they've got one thing to do and it brings them through a process. So yeah, almost everything else, logos, videos, all those things hurt this page pretty bad. Okay, step number three and this is the big key to this whole thing. If you look at what Facebook is doing right now, if you look at Google, you look at any of the ad networks, ad networks do not want you ever promoting landing pages and webinar registration pages. They did not serve them at all. What ad networks want is they want you to promote content. That's what serves them. Okay, someone comes to Facebook and clicks on an article and gives a good article. That helps serve, that makes the user experience better for Facebook. And all of us, I've been for 12 years kicking against the pricks, fighting this because I'm just like, no, I want people to go to my landing page. I want their email address, right? It doesn't serve the networks. So eventually either we keep fighting or we have to conform. And what's interesting is that we're finding that conforming is actually increasing conversions so now I'm okay conforming with it. And so this is the process. We talk a lot in dot com secrets and everything about value ladders. What's happening before the value ladder? What gets somebody into the value ladder? This is the content. This is inbound marketing. Okay, this is the big buzzword right now. In fact, HubSpot did a big event last month. They're inbound marketing. Anyone go to that? Anyone see anything about that? All you guys are direct response marketers like me. We just want to sell crap to people, right? The new way, the future of where things are going is content, right? They didn't even have over 10,000 people at this thing all talking about inbound marketing, okay? Which is creating content and then promoting that content. So instead of promoting your squeeze page or your landing page, it's creating amazing content and then promoting that because that's what Facebook wants you to do. If you don't want to get showed on my Facebook, don't promote a webinar registration page. Promote content and then get someone to come to the webinar registration page. And if you do it correctly, what I'm going to show you guys today, this content is a pre-frame to the other thing. So it doesn't matter. Actually, if you do it right, it'll actually increase your conversions. It doesn't hurt your conversions. That makes sense? Okay, so if you look at like the big, what I think where we all need to be focusing over next year, two years, five years is this. Like it's this. This is where all the networks want us to go. They're forcing us to go and we can fight against it and keep losing Facebook accounts day in and day out or we can learn it and use it as a tool and it'll be all right. It's all going to be all right. We hired Neil Patel. And you guys follow Neil Patel? So he's in my space, he's the guy who's doing this the best and I wanted to kind of pick his brain and learn. And I was like, I'm going to pay him a whole crap ton of money, I'm going to have to boil you for a day and just like, just pick his brain on this, right? And what's interesting about a lot of this inbound stuff, it's kind of frustrating at first because like, how many of you guys blog? Do you guys try to? How many of you guys like, you post a blog post and then you see how much money come in? A couple people. Yeah, it sucks, huh? It's horrible. And I was asking Neil, we've blown it out of my head. This is the deal. I believe it inbound now. I've been doing this for 12 years, I finally believe it. Like, what's the process? Like, this is how it works. Every day, you got to write a blog post. Like, cool, then what? Like, then the next morning you wake up and you got to write another blog post. I'm like, okay, then what? The next day you wake up and write another blog post. I'm like, okay, well, when does the money come? He's like, it doesn't, for a long time. But in about a year from now, you have more traffic you can even handle. But you got to be consistent for a year. I'm like, that sucks. I don't like that. He's like, well, that's how it works. And then he said, guess how many options a day I get right now for free? Like how many? He said, 1,700 options a day he gets for free. I was like, oh, okay. I'm willing to focus for a little bit. 700 options a day? How many do you get for your webinar each week on average? Okay, if you have that every day for free and you paid zero for it, would that have been worth, like, the last year worth the work? Yeah, so there's value. There's some things that are going to suck for a little bit for all of us. I'm the same thing. Like it's not immediate cash all the time. Now, tomorrow we're going to talk about how do we promote this content and there are ways that you can amplify and you can speed up and make money faster. Because I don't know about you, but I like money faster. So there's things you can do and we're going to continue to do. But understanding that this is like, this is kind of a longer strategy. So for this to work, so it's understanding inbound. Now the other cool thing with inbound, why I want to stress on this, is because... We're going hot, warm to cold, right? So I'm going cold, and I know who's one of my dream clients I would like to go after. So let's just say, for example, and then just throw it ahead. Let's say I want to get more chiropractors and click funnels. So what I can do is I can focus on inbound, create things that are inbound, that are just talking to chiropractors about this thing. I can put it in places that chiropractors are at. And they come in cold, and this inbound is what warms them up and brings them into my funnels. Or if I know I want to go after financial planners. I create content that speaks to those people, where they're at currently. We put it out there, we promote it, and that brings people into my funnels. So it gives me the ability to splinter out who I'm bringing into my world without me creating new funnels every single time. I just want to create one funnel focused there and then create different content that brings them people, converts them from cold to warm to hot, and brings them into my funnel, if that makes sense. So you'll see this again as we dig deeper into it. I know there's a lot of stuff I'm setting up for later, but hopefully you guys understand that it's all a framework. It's in place and you're like, oh, that's how it all works together. Different strategies. Good question. So this question was better to write crappy blog posts every day or really good ones a week. We take them through this funnel catcher process, right? Where we are promoting to the cold people where they're at. Okay, so this is where I'm at right now. I promote, I push them to a content platform where I have something that speaks to this person. They go from there to a pre-frame bridge into my funnel. Okay, and that helps them to become hot. And they give me tons of money every day for the rest of my life. Okay, so look at this. This is the process, right? Unaware, problem-aware. So you're finding people that are unaware. The content makes them problem-aware. Pre-bridge makes them solution-aware. Final makes them product-aware. And then they give you a bunch of money.
